citations.description: Content present at the actual path, not the path named in the criterion
list_directory('examples') returned only 'agent-skills/' — no 'claude-skills/' directory exists. search_files for 'claude-skills' found no file at that path.

findings.criterion: `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md` contains a new subsection instructing the agent to read `docs/plan-review-gate.md` and `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` before recording tickets.
findings.detail: The path `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md` does not exist in the repository. The directory `examples/claude-skills/` does not exist at all — the actual brainstorm skill lives at `examples/agent-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md`. While that file does contain the required subsection at lines 124–137, the criterion names a specific file path that is absent.
findings.remediation: Either update the AC to reference `examples/agent-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md` (the actual path) and confirm the criterion is met, or verify that the directory was renamed and the ticket's path was simply stale.

citations.description: Duplicate ticket c681 file_impact: [{"path": "examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md", "reason": "add pre-recording plan-review-gate note"}] — but content is at examples/agent-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md

findings.criterion: The change is docs-only: `git diff --stat origin/main` shows only that one SKILL.md file changed.
findings.detail: This criterion requires running `git diff --stat origin/main`, which is not possible with the available read-only file tools. No git metadata is accessible to confirm that only one file changed. Additionally, the file_impact entry on the duplicate ticket c681 names `examples/claude-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md`, while the content actually appears at `examples/agent-skills/rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md`, raising a question about which change actually landed and whether the diff scope is as stated.
findings.remediation: Run `git diff --stat origin/main` and record the output as a ticket comment to demonstrate only one SKILL.md changed.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
